seo.title: About Us | FAS-Technology
seo.description: FAS-Tech develops certified traction hoists and suspended access equipment for contractors, elevator installers, and façade access professionals worldwide.

story.imageAlt: FAS-Tech traction hoist installation on a commercial jobsite
paragraphs: FAS-Tech develops certified traction hoists, suspended access equipment, and lifting solutions for contractors, rental companies, elevator installers, and façade access professionals worldwide. We combine European engineering, controlled manufacturing, and practical jobsite experience to deliver equipment that is safe, robust, and straightforward to maintain.

Our support extends beyond the product. From equipment selection and project engineering to technical documentation, training, spare parts, and after-sales service, we help customers keep their crews working and their projects moving.
For the North American market, FAS-Tech provides UL and CSA-certified solutions designed around local power supplies, platform configurations, and jobsite requirements.

items.label: CERTIFIED SAFETY
items.line: Equipment tested and certified to applicable North American and European standards, supported by documented quality controls and production inspections.

items.label: JOBSITE UPTIME
items.line: Robust equipment designed for demanding environments, familiar maintenance procedures, standard tools, and straightforward access to service parts.

items.label: RESPONSIVE SUPPORT
items.line: Application guidance, technical documentation, training, troubleshooting, and after-sales assistance throughout the equipment lifecycle.

certifications.heading: Certifications
certifications.intro: Safety and regulatory compliance are built into every UNILIFT traction hoist. Our North American and European models are tested and certified to the standards governing powered suspended access equipment in their respective markets.

items.description: UNILIFT is certified to UL 1323, the U.S. safety standard for portable hoists used on suspended scaffolds and swing stages. The standard covers construction, electrical safety, mechanical performance, braking systems, and operational reliability.
For the Canadian market, UNILIFT complies with CSA Z271, the standard governing the design, manufacture, testing, and safe operation of suspended access equipment.
European UNILIFT models comply with the essential health and safety requirements of the Machinery Directive 2006/42/EC and carry CE marking for placement on the European market.

UNILIFT is certified to EN 1808:2015, the European standard for suspended access equipment. It covers design, construction, stability, safety devices, controls, testing, and protection against mechanical and electrical risks.
